title: Next Steps in SOA: Applications on the Network
author: Peter S. Kastner
subject_domain: SOA; Network Architecture; XML appliances; Application distribution
methodology: Aberdeen 'Business Value Point of View' research prospectus; survey-based study announcement with methodology description
abstract: Aberdeen 'Business Value Point of View' paper and study prospectus for the third in an ongoing SOA research series. Argues that SOA distributes and decouples processing from the data center to the network edge. As traffic shifts from data packets to XML-based content routing and Web Services messaging, network and software architecture lines blur. SOA middleware is now delivered inside XML-aware appliances; network infrastructure is taking on new roles; a new generation of SOA-aware software is emerging. Research will benchmark application processing on the network and survey IT organizations with one-on-one interviews; reach: 1M+ value chain executives via Aberdeen's Global 5000 network and affiliate channels. Timeframe: Q1 2006.
importance_rationale: Short prospectus/announcement document rather than a full research report; limited primary data. Captures Aberdeen's framing of network-as-SOA-platform thesis in early 2006.
relevance_rationale: Directly addresses SOA architecture evolution — specifically the move of middleware into network appliances and the blurring of network/software architecture boundaries. Core SOA-era thesis.
prescience_rationale: The vision of SOA middleware in XML-aware appliances, applications distributed to network edge, and network infrastructure taking on new application processing roles accurately anticipates CDNs, API gateways, edge computing, and cloud-native networking patterns.
